# Auto-Generated OG Images Implementation Plan

> **For agentic workers:** REQUIRED SUB-SKILL: Use superpowers:subagent-driven-development (recommended) or superpowers:executing-plans to implement this plan task-by-task. Steps use checkbox (`- [ ]`) syntax for tracking.

**Goal:** Generate a unique, branded OG image per blog post using Playwright screenshots of an HTML template.

**Architecture:** A Node script reads post frontmatter, renders an HTML template with series-specific gradients and the post title, and screenshots it to PNG at 1200x630. Hugo serves these via an opengraph partial override that checks for per-post images before falling back to the default.

**Tech Stack:** Node.js, Playwright, gray-matter, Hugo/PaperMod

**Spec:** `docs/superpowers/specs/2026-03-21-og-images-design.md`

---

## File Map

| File | Action | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| `package.json` | Create | Dev dependencies (playwright, gray-matter) |
| `scripts/og-template.html` | Create | HTML template rendered to PNG (1200x630) |
| `scripts/generate-og-images.js` | Create | Scans posts, renders template, screenshots to PNG |
| `layouts/partials/templates/opengraph.html` | Create | Override PaperMod's partial to use per-post OG images |
| `Taskfile.yml` | Modify | Add `og:generate` and `og:regenerate` tasks |
| `.gitignore` | Modify | Add `.superpowers/` |
| `static/images/og/` | Create dir | Output directory for generated PNGs |

---

### Task 1: Project Setup

**Files:**
- Create: `package.json`
- Modify: `.gitignore`
- Create dir: `static/images/og/`

- [ ] **Step 1: Initialize npm and install dependencies**

```bash
npm init -y
npm install --save-dev playwright gray-matter
npx playwright install chromium
```

- [ ] **Step 2: Add `.superpowers/` to `.gitignore`**

Append to `.gitignore`:
```
# Superpowers brainstorm sessions
.superpowers/
```

- [ ] **Step 3: Create output directory**

```bash
mkdir -p static/images/og
```

- [ ] **Step 4: Commit**

```bash
git add package.json package-lock.json .gitignore static/images/og/
git commit -m "chore: add npm setup for OG image generation"
```

---

### Task 2: HTML Template

**Files:**
- Create: `scripts/og-template.html`

- [ ] **Step 1: Create the OG image HTML template**

Create `scripts/og-template.html` — a self-contained HTML document at exactly 1200x630px. The template uses placeholder markers that the script will replace:

- `{{gradient}}` — CSS linear-gradient value
- `{{series}}` — series display label
- `{{title}}` — post title
- `{{fontSize}}` — font size in px (64, 48, or 36)
- `{{author}}` — "Russell Jones"

```html
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html>
<head>
<meta charset="utf-8">
<style>
  * { margin: 0; padding: 0; box-sizing: border-box; }
  body {
    width: 1200px;
    height: 630px;
    font-family: -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, 'Segoe UI', Roboto, sans-serif;
    overflow: hidden;
  }
  .container {
    width: 1200px;
    height: 630px;
    background: linear-gradient(135deg, {{gradient}});
    display: flex;
    flex-direction: column;
    justify-content: center;
    padding: 60px 80px;
    position: relative;
    overflow: hidden;
  }
  .circle-1 {
    position: absolute;
    top: -40px;
    right: -40px;
    width: 280px;
    height: 280px;
    border: 4px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.12);
    border-radius: 50%;
  }
  .circle-2 {
    position: absolute;
    bottom: -60px;
    left: -60px;
    width: 220px;
    height: 220px;
    border: 4px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.08);
    border-radius: 50%;
  }
  .badge {
    display: inline-block;
    background: rgba(255,255,255,0.2);
    color: white;
    font-size: 14px;
    font-weight: 700;
    padding: 6px 14px;
    border-radius: 4px;
    text-transform: uppercase;
    letter-spacing: 1.5px;
    margin-bottom: 24px;
    width: fit-content;
  }
  .title {
    color: white;
    font-size: {{fontSize}}px;
    font-weight: 800;
    line-height: 1.2;
    max-width: 90%;
  }
  .author {
    color: rgba(255,255,255,0.7);
    font-size: 16px;
    margin-top: 24px;
  }
</style>
</head>
<body>
  <div class="container">
    <div class="circle-1"></div>
    <div class="circle-2"></div>
    <div class="badge">{{series}}</div>
    <div class="title">{{title}}</div>
    <div class="author">{{author}}</div>
  </div>
</body>
</html>
```

- [ ] **Step 2: Verify template renders in a browser**

Open `scripts/og-template.html` in a browser manually. Confirm it renders at 1200x630 with gradient, circles, badge, title, and author. The placeholders will show as literal text — that's expected.

- [ ] **Step 3: Commit**

```bash
git add scripts/og-template.html
git commit -m "feat: add OG image HTML template"
```

---

### Task 3: Generation Script

**Files:**
- Create: `scripts/generate-og-images.js`

- [ ] **Step 1: Write the generation script**

Create `scripts/generate-og-images.js`:

```javascript
const { chromium } = require('playwright');
const matter = require('gray-matter');
const fs = require('fs');
const path = require('path');
const crypto = require('crypto');
const { glob } = require('fs').promises;

const SERIES_MAP = {
  'waaseyaa':          { gradient: '#667eea, #764ba2',   label: 'Waaseyaa' },
  'php-fig-standards': { gradient: '#0f9b8e, #1a5276',   label: 'PHP-FIG Standards' },
  'codified-context':  { gradient: '#f093fb, #f5576c',   label: 'Codified Context' },
  'production-linux':  { gradient: '#e65100, #bf360c',   label: 'Production Linux' },
  '_default':          { gradient: '#2c3e50, #4ca1af',   label: 'Blog' },
};

const TEMPLATE_PATH = path.join(__dirname, 'og-template.html');
const OUTPUT_DIR = path.join(__dirname, '..', 'static', 'images', 'og');
const HASH_FILE = path.join(OUTPUT_DIR, '.og-template-hash');

function getFontSize(title) {
  if (title.length < 40) return 64;
  if (title.length <= 80) return 48;
  return 36;
}

function getSeriesInfo(series) {
  if (!series || series.length === 0) return SERIES_MAP['_default'];
  const key = Array.isArray(series) ? series[0] : series;
  return SERIES_MAP[key] || SERIES_MAP['_default'];
}

function findPosts() {
  const postsDir = path.join(__dirname, '..', 'content', 'posts');
  const posts = [];

  function walk(dir) {
    for (const entry of fs.readdirSync(dir, { withFileTypes: true })) {
      const full = path.join(dir, entry.name);
      if (entry.isDirectory()) {
        walk(full);
      } else if (entry.name === 'index.md') {
        const raw = fs.readFileSync(full, 'utf-8');
        const { data } = matter(raw);
        if (data.slug && data.title) {
          posts.push({
            slug: data.slug,
            title: data.title,
            series: data.series || [],
          });
        }
      }
    }
  }

  walk(postsDir);
  return posts;
}

async function main() {
  const force = process.argv.includes('--force');

  // Ensure output directory exists
  fs.mkdirSync(OUTPUT_DIR, { recursive: true });

  // Read template and compute hash
  const template = fs.readFileSync(TEMPLATE_PATH, 'utf-8');
  const templateHash = crypto.createHash('sha256').update(template).digest('hex');

  // Check if template changed
  let regenerateAll = force;
  if (!force && fs.existsSync(HASH_FILE)) {
    const oldHash = fs.readFileSync(HASH_FILE, 'utf-8').trim();
    if (oldHash !== templateHash) {
      console.log('Template changed — regenerating all images');
      regenerateAll = true;
    }
  } else if (!force) {
    regenerateAll = true; // No hash file yet, generate all
  }

  // Find posts and filter
  const posts = findPosts();
  const toGenerate = regenerateAll
    ? posts
    : posts.filter(p => !fs.existsSync(path.join(OUTPUT_DIR, `${p.slug}.png`)));

  if (toGenerate.length === 0) {
    console.log('All OG images up to date');
    return;
  }

  console.log(`Generating ${toGenerate.length} OG image(s)...`);

  // Launch browser
  const browser = await chromium.launch();
  const page = await browser.newPage();
  await page.setViewportSize({ width: 1200, height: 630 });

  for (const post of toGenerate) {
    const info = getSeriesInfo(post.series);
    const fontSize = getFontSize(post.title);

    const html = template
      .replace(/\{\{gradient\}\}/g, info.gradient)
      .replace(/\{\{series\}\}/g, info.label)
      .replace(/\{\{title\}\}/g, post.title.replace(/&/g, '&amp;').replace(/</g, '&lt;'))
      .replace(/\{\{fontSize\}\}/g, String(fontSize))
      .replace(/\{\{author\}\}/g, 'Russell Jones');

    await page.setContent(html, { waitUntil: 'load' });
    const outputPath = path.join(OUTPUT_DIR, `${post.slug}.png`);
    await page.screenshot({ path: outputPath, type: 'png' });
    console.log(`  ✓ ${post.slug}.png`);
  }

  await browser.close();

  // Write template hash
  fs.writeFileSync(HASH_FILE, templateHash);
  console.log('Done');
}

main().catch(err => {
  console.error(err);
  process.exit(1);
});
```

- [ ] **Step 2: Run the script to generate all images**

```bash
node scripts/generate-og-images.js
```

Expected: prints each slug as it generates, creates PNGs in `static/images/og/`, writes `.og-template-hash`.

- [ ] **Step 3: Verify a generated image**

Open any PNG from `static/images/og/` (e.g., `waaseyaa-dbal-migration.png`) and confirm:
- 1200x630 dimensions
- Correct gradient for the series
- Title is readable
- Series badge and author name are present
- Geometric circles visible

- [ ] **Step 4: Run again without `--force` to verify incremental mode**

```bash
node scripts/generate-og-images.js
```

Expected: "All OG images up to date" (no regeneration).

- [ ] **Step 5: Commit**

```bash
git add scripts/generate-og-images.js
git commit -m "feat: add OG image generation script"
```

---

### Task 4: Hugo Partial Override

**Files:**
- Create: `layouts/partials/templates/opengraph.html`

- [ ] **Step 1: Create the opengraph partial override**

Copy PaperMod's `themes/PaperMod/layouts/partials/templates/opengraph.html` to `layouts/partials/templates/opengraph.html`. Then modify the image section (lines 38-50) to check for a per-post OG image first.

Replace:
```html
{{- if .Params.cover.image -}}
  {{- if (ne .Params.cover.relative true) }}
    <meta property="og:image" content="{{ .Params.cover.image | absURL }}">
  {{- else}}
    <meta property="og:image" content="{{ (path.Join .RelPermalink .Params.cover.image ) | absURL }}">
  {{- end}}
{{- else }}
  {{- with partial "_funcs/get-page-images" . }}
    {{- range . | first 6 }}
      <meta property="og:image" content="{{ .Permalink }}">
    {{- end }}
  {{- end }}
{{- end }}
```

With:
```html
{{- $ogImage := printf "static/images/og/%s.png" .Slug -}}
{{- if fileExists $ogImage -}}
  <meta property="og:image" content="{{ printf "images/og/%s.png" .Slug | absURL }}">
{{- else if .Params.cover.image -}}
  {{- if (ne .Params.cover.relative true) }}
    <meta property="og:image" content="{{ .Params.cover.image | absURL }}">
  {{- else}}
    <meta property="og:image" content="{{ (path.Join .RelPermalink .Params.cover.image ) | absURL }}">
  {{- end}}
{{- else }}
  {{- with partial "_funcs/get-page-images" . }}
    {{- range . | first 6 }}
      <meta property="og:image" content="{{ .Permalink }}">
    {{- end }}
  {{- end }}
{{- end }}
```

- [ ] **Step 2: Build and verify the og:image tag**

```bash
hugo --gc --minify
grep -r 'og:image' public/waaseyaa-dbal-migration/index.html
```

Expected: `<meta property="og:image" content="https://jonesrussell.github.io/blog/images/og/waaseyaa-dbal-migration.png">`

- [ ] **Step 3: Verify fallback for posts without OG images**

Delete one PNG temporarily, rebuild, and check that the fallback `og-default.png` is used:

```bash
mv static/images/og/waaseyaa-dbal-migration.png /tmp/
hugo --gc --minify
grep 'og:image' public/waaseyaa-dbal-migration/index.html
mv /tmp/waaseyaa-dbal-migration.png static/images/og/
```

Expected: fallback shows `og-default.png`.

- [ ] **Step 4: Commit**

```bash
git add layouts/partials/templates/opengraph.html
git commit -m "feat: override opengraph partial for per-post OG images"
```

---

### Task 5: Taskfile Integration

**Files:**
- Modify: `Taskfile.yml`

- [ ] **Step 1: Add og tasks to Taskfile.yml**

Add after the `deploy` task:

```yaml
  og:generate:
    desc: Generate OG images for posts missing them (incremental)
    cmds:
      - node scripts/generate-og-images.js

  og:regenerate:
    desc: Force regenerate all OG images
    cmds:
      - node scripts/generate-og-images.js --force
```

- [ ] **Step 2: Verify tasks work**

```bash
task og:generate
```

Expected: "All OG images up to date" (since they were already generated).

- [ ] **Step 3: Commit**

```bash
git add Taskfile.yml
git commit -m "feat: add og:generate and og:regenerate tasks"
```

---

### Task 6: Commit Generated Images and Final Verification

**Files:**
- Add: `static/images/og/*.png`
- Add: `static/images/og/.og-template-hash`

- [ ] **Step 1: Verify image count matches post count**

```bash
ls static/images/og/*.png | wc -l
find content/posts -name index.md | wc -l
```

These numbers should be close (some posts may lack slugs).

- [ ] **Step 2: Full build verification**

```bash
task build
```

Expected: clean build, no errors.

- [ ] **Step 3: Spot-check several posts' og:image tags**

```bash
grep 'og:image' public/waaseyaa-i18n/index.html
grep 'og:image' public/waaseyaa-intro/index.html
grep 'og:image' public/codified-context-the-problem/index.html
```

Each should reference its own `images/og/{slug}.png` with the full absolute URL.

- [ ] **Step 4: Commit all generated images**

```bash
git add static/images/og/
git commit -m "feat: add generated OG images for all posts"
```

- [ ] **Step 5: Add `.superpowers/` to `.gitignore` and commit**

```bash
git add .gitignore
git commit -m "chore: ignore .superpowers/ directory"
```
